page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a single local NVMe SSD with a capacity of 402 GB, while no dedicated GPUs are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. However, memory bandwidth exhibits notable performance trade-offs; while cached small-block read operations are average, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak to poor, with large-block writes falling into the bottom 10%.

Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and local NVMe SSD storage, this server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space, enhancing its qualitative cost efficiency for I/O-sensitive tasks that do not require massive memory arrays or GPU acceleration. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or memory-intensive uncached data processing due to its lower large-block memory bandwidth.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD. It features 8 physical cores with multithreading and lacks a GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core operations, database tasks, and web serving throughput perform in the average range. A key hardware trade-off is its large-block uncached memory write bandwidth, which falls into the bottom 10%. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources and fast local storage, such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, but is less optimal for memory-bandwidth-intensive applications or large-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is a general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for I/O-sensitive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor
64 GB of system memory providing 4 GB per vCPU
Bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database workloads
Reduced performance on large-block uncached memory write operations
